growth_records.medium: succinate-based medium (photosynthetic growth); exact medium (standard succinate medium vs M2SF two-carbon medium) not specified for this parameter
growth_records.cite: Klamt et al. Modeling the electron transport chain of purple non-sulfur bacteria. Molecular Systems Biology (2008). DOI:10.1038/msb4100191. [PMC2238716]
growth_records.snippet: mue_max ... 0.17 h-1 ... Maximal growth rate (for R. rubrum; from own experiments; see also Grammel et al (2003) ...)
growth_records.notes: Value taken directly from Table IV (kinetic model parameters) as the maximal growth rate parameter 'mue_max' for R. rubrum, stated to derive from the authors' own experiments (cross-referenced to Grammel et al 2003). No specific medium composition, temperature or pH is given alongside this parameter in the main text; paper describes R. rubrum grown photosynthetically on succinate (standard medium) or on the M2SF medium (succinate+fructose) developed by Ghosh et al (1994), but does not state which of these corresponds to the 0.17 h-1 value. No calculation performed; value is reported verbatim.

up.units: n/a
up.method: HPLC (fructose uptake curve, Fig 1A; numeric rate only in figure)

sec.units: % w/w of CDW (max at 108 h)

yields.biomass_note: grew from ~1.2 to 2.2 g/l at 132 h

growth_records.cite: Manyu Tang et al. The metabolic pathways of carbon assimilation and polyhydroxyalkanoate production by Rhodospirillum rubrum in response to different atmospheric fermentation. PLoS One (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0306222. [PMC11268599]
growth_records.snippet: the cultures with 10 g l−1 fructose had grown to the highest concentration (2.2 g l−1). And the highest PHA content of 24.4% (w/w) was detected at 108 h... approximately 53.5% 3HB and 46.5% 3HV monomers.
growth_records.notes: Batch flask fructose fermentation. Max biomass 2.2 g/l (from ~1.2 g/l initial). No clean exponential mu extractable (slow PHA-accumulation growth over 132 h with lag); growth_rate left null. Fructose uptake rate curve shown in Fig 1A but numeric values not in text. PHBV 24.4% w/w at 108 h, monomers 53.5% 3HB/46.5% 3HV. Non-strictly-anaerobic (dark, shaken), hence microaerophilic.

growth_records.medium: RRNCO medium + 10 mM sodium acetate, 100% CO gas atmosphere

up.units: % of supplied CO consumed (~25% conversion over 12 d)
n/a (co-substrate, consumed; per Fig 8D acetate to 43% by day 6 in pure CO bioreactor)

sec.units: % w/w of CDW (max at 12 d)

yields.biomass_note: biomass stayed at initial ~1.2 g/l (no net growth on CO)

growth_records.cite: Manyu Tang et al. The metabolic pathways of carbon assimilation and polyhydroxyalkanoate production by Rhodospirillum rubrum in response to different atmospheric fermentation. PLoS One (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0306222. [PMC11268599]
growth_records.snippet: although the biomass was maintained at the initial value of 1.2 g l−1 in the CO cultivation, a similar maximum PHBV production (23.3%) was obtained after 12 d... the content of 3HV in this PHBV was only 2.2%... the CO conversion was around
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle pure-CO photoheterotrophic run. No net biomass growth (held ~1.2 g/l), so no growth rate. CO conversion ~25% over 12 d (Fig 1B). PHBV 23.3% w/w, 3HV 2.2%. Acetate 10 mM co-substrate consumed. Uptake reported as % conversion, not gDW-normalized flux.

growth_records.medium: RRNCO medium + 10 mM acetate, 50% CO / 50% H2 syngas atmosphere

up.units: % of supplied CO assimilated by 8 d (complete assimilation)
g/l/d (max acetate consumption rate, 3 d after inoculation, CO/H2 atmosphere)

sec.units: % w/w (PHA decreased with increasing H2; 3HV molar proportion rose, up to 11.8% at 80% H2)

yields.CO_conversion_pct: 100 (fully assimilated by day 8 at 50% H2); 42 at 20% H2

growth_records.cite: Manyu Tang et al. The metabolic pathways of carbon assimilation and polyhydroxyalkanoate production by Rhodospirillum rubrum in response to different atmospheric fermentation. PLoS One (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0306222. [PMC11268599]
growth_records.snippet: when the H2/CO ratio was increased to 50%, CO was completely assimilated after 8 d of incubation... The CO absorption speed was largely accelerated by adding 20% hydrogen and the CO conversion was enhanced to 42%... the maximum values of ac
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle syngas (50% CO/50% H2) photoheterotrophic. H2 addition accelerated CO uptake: 20% H2 -> 42% CO conversion; 50% H2 -> 100% CO assimilated by 8 d; 80% H2 -> 3HV molar fraction 11.8%. Max acetate consumption 0.3 g/l/d (directly reported). Derived (not reported): 0.3 g/l/d acetate ÷ 60.05 g/mol = 5.0 mmol/l/d = 0.208 mmol/l/h volumetric; not gDW-normalized because time-varying biomass. No growth rate (biomass not resolved per H2 ratio).

up.units: % of 2.4 L CO consumed by day 12 (20.38% by day 6)
mmol/gDW/h (DERIVED, time-averaged to day 12)
% of 10 mM consumed by day 6 (Table 3)

sec.units: % w/w CDW at day 12 (7.90% at day 6)

growth_records.cite: Manyu Tang et al. The metabolic pathways of carbon assimilation and polyhydroxyalkanoate production by Rhodospirillum rubrum in response to different atmospheric fermentation. PLoS One (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0306222. [PMC11268599]
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch bioreactor Table 3 (CDW g/l, PHBV %ww, HV%, CO consumption %, acetate consumption %). DERIVED CO uptake (not reported): 2.4 L CO x 62.86% = 1.509 L consumed by day 12; n=PV/RT=(1 atm x 1.509 L)/(0.08206 x 301 K)=0.0611 mol=61.1 mmol; final CDW 2.95 g/l x 0.6 l = 1.77 gDW; over 12 d (288 h): 61.1/1.77/288 = 0.120 mmol CO/gDW/h (time-averaged, endpoint biomass). Day-6 equivalent: 2.4x0.2038=0.489 L=19.8 mmol; 2.32 g/l x0.6=1.39 gDW; /144 h = 0.099 mmol/gDW/h. No exponential mu reported.

growth_records.cite: Manyu Tang et al. The metabolic pathways of carbon assimilation and polyhydroxyalkanoate production by Rhodospirillum rubrum in response to different atmospheric fermentation. PLoS One (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0306222. [PMC11268599]
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch syngas (75%CO/25%H2) Table 3. H2 addition halved fermentation time vs pure CO (6 vs 12 d) and raised 3HV (~6.2-6.3% vs 2.2-2.4%). DERIVED CO uptake (not reported): 1.8 L x 50.20% = 0.904 L by day 6; n=0.904/24.70=0.0366 mol=36.6 mmol; CDW 2.65 g/l x0.6=1.59 gDW; over 6 d (144 h): 36.6/1.59/144 = 0.160 mmol CO/gDW/h. Day-4: 1.8x0.2406=0.433 L=17.5 mmol; 2.45x0.6=1.47 gDW; /96 h = 0.124 mmol/gDW/h. RT term=0.08206 L·atm/mol/K x 301 K = 24.70.

growth_records.cite: Karmann, Stephanie et al. Tailor-made PAT platform for safe syngas fermentations in batch, fed-batch and chemostat mode with Rhodospirillum rubrum. Microbial Biotechnology (2017). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.12727. [PMC5658627]
growth_records.snippet: During the batch phase, the cells grew exponentially at maximal specific growth rate mu_max of 0.1 h-1 (based on the OD600) until CO limitation... At the end of the fed-batch (after 184 h), an OD600 of 23.5 and a cell dry weight (CDW) of 5.
growth_records.notes: mu_max=0.1 h-1 reported (OD600-based, batch phase). Doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.1 = 6.93 h (calculated). CO uptake and H2/CO2 production rates were measured online by QMS (experimental, is_experimental true) but numeric values appear only in Figure 3, not in text, so rate=null. Acetic acid feed set-point 0.17 mmol OD600-1 L-1 h-1 is an empirical feed rate (not a measured specific uptake). Anaerobic CO metabolism (water-gas shift: CO + H2O -> CO2 + H2 via CODH). Preculture separately on modified RRNCO + 15 mM fructose, 30C, 180 rpm, 46-48 h.

growth_records.cite: Karmann, Stephanie et al. Tailor-made PAT platform for safe syngas fermentations in batch, fed-batch and chemostat mode with Rhodospirillum rubrum. Microbial Biotechnology (2017). DOI:10.1111/1751-7915.12727. [PMC5658627]
growth_records.snippet: the continuous mode was started by constantly adding fresh modified RRNCO medium supplemented with 10 mM acetic acid at a dilution rate of D = 0.02 h-1... the OD600 was stable at 3.3 +/- 0.04, the CDW at 0.67 +/- 0.02 g l-1 and the intracel
growth_records.notes: At chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.02 h-1 (calculated). Doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.02 = 34.66 h. CDW 0.67+/-0.02 g/L, OD600 3.3+/-0.04, PHB 4.7 wt% (all reported). CO uptake and H2/CO2 production measured online (QMS) and reached constant plateau at steady state but numeric values only in Figure 4. Acetic acid added at 0.2 g/h separate from 0.02 h-1 medium feed.

growth_records.medium: modified RRNCO medium + syngas + acetate (batch/exponential phase)

up.units: consumed to below detection limit (<3 uM CO) ~30 h after inoculation despite continuous sparging
0.59 g/L initial acetate consumed during batch phase (0-43 h)

sec.units: increase in off-gas concomitant with DCO consumption (water-gas shift)
increase in off-gas concomitant with DCO consumption (water-gas shift)

growth_records.cite: Karmann et al. Fed-Batch Cultivations of Rhodospirillum rubrum Under Multiple Nutrient-Limited Growth Conditions on Syngas as a Novel Option to Produce Poly(3-Hydroxybutyrate) (PHB). Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00059. [PMC6454858]
growth_records.snippet: Exponential (unlimited) growth was observed between 5 and 30 h of cultivation in all bioreactor experiments showing a maximum specific growth rate of mu_max = 0.1 +/- 0.01 h-1 based on OD600 and on TCC measurements
growth_records.notes: Batch/exponential phase common to all three fed-batch runs (C-, C,N-, C,P-limited); bioreactor syngas during runs = 25% CO, 25% H2, 5% CO2, 45% N2 v/v sparged at 0.1 L/L/min. mu_max=0.1+/-0.01/h reported (OD600 & TCC). td=ln2/0.1=6.93 h (calculated). DCO consumed below 3 uM by ~30 h (myoglobin assay). CO/H2/CO2 off-gas patterns follow water-gas shift (CODH). Biomass/OD conversion: 4.45e8 cells/mL per OD600 unit; 1.5e12 cells/g biomass. Preculture on fructose 2.7 g/L (15 mM). Note: literature-cited (NOT measured here) doubling times vs CO content from Kerby et al. 1995 = 4.8, 5.7, 8.4 h for 25, 50, 100 CO% - not curated as this-paper data. Serum-vial batch tests (100% vs 60% syngas + 0.59 g/L acetate) gave linear (not exponential) growth, max OD600 3.07+/-0.37 at 60% syngas vs 0.73+/-0.1 at 100% syngas; no mu measured there. Full RRNCO recipe references Kerby et al. 1995 / Karmann et al. 2017 (not fully enumerated in this paper).

growth_records.medium: modified RRNCO medium + syngas + acetate feed (C-limited fed-batch)

up.units: g CO/gDW/h (approx; specific CO consumption rate at the growth/energy-limiting point, corresponds to maintenance energy ~143 J/gDW/h from -20 kJ/mol water-gas shift)
up.method: off-gas MS + dissolved-CO balance; described as growth-limiting specific rate

up.units: mg acetate/L/h (volumetric, DERIVED)
up.method: calculated from volumetric biomass productivity / Y_X/acetate

sec.units: mg/gDW/h (specific PHB productivity q_PHB, phase iii)
mg/L/h (volumetric PHB productivity, phase iii)
mg/L/h (volumetric biomass productivity, phase iii, ~30 mg/L/h for ~100 h)
Y_CO2/CO = 0.589 g/g (approx 60% mol/mol of CO consumed released as CO2); off-gas
Y_H2/CO = 0.042 g/g (off-gas, water-gas shift)

growth_records.cite: Karmann et al. Fed-Batch Cultivations of Rhodospirillum rubrum Under Multiple Nutrient-Limited Growth Conditions on Syngas as a Novel Option to Produce Poly(3-Hydroxybutyrate) (PHB). Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00059. [PMC6454858]
growth_records.snippet: the CDW continued to increase for about 100 h at a rate of almost 30 mg L-1 h-1, whereas the specific PHB productivity was 2.2 mg g-1 h-1 and the volumetric PHB productivity was at 2.9 mg L-1 h-1 during this period... final CDW 5.1 +/- 0.09
growth_records.notes: C-limited fed-batch, phase (iii). Directly reported: volumetric biomass productivity ~30 mg/L/h; q_PHB=2.2 mg/gDW/h; volumetric PHB=2.9 mg/L/h; final CDW 5.1+/-0.09 g/L; PHB 0.59 g/L (11.9% w/w); TCC 3.6e12 /L. Yields (Table 2, whole process except N-yield for 0-100 h): Y_X/acetate=0.628, Y_X/CO=0.036, Y_PHB/acetate=0.072, Y_PHB/CO=0.004, Y_H2/CO=0.042, Y_CO2/CO=0.589, Y_X/N=17.40 (all g/g). DERIVED volumetric acetate uptake = biomass productivity / Y_X/acetate = 30 mg/L/h / 0.628 = 47.8 mg acetate/L/h (marked is_experimental:false). Specific CO consumption ~0.2 g CO/gDW/h is the growth-limiting/maintenance value (energy: -20 kJ/mol water-gas shift -> ~143 J/gDW/h maintenance). q_PHB in molar = 2.2 mg/gDW/h / 86 g/mol = 0.0256 mmol/gDW/h (3HB MW ~86). mu=0.1/h and td=6.93 h are from the shared batch phase (not re-measured within phase iii).

up.method: calculated from volumetric biomass productivity / Y_X/acetate

Y_X/CO = 0.020 g/g (CO consumed; specific rate not tabulated)

sec.units: mg/gDW/h (specific PHB productivity q_PHB, phase iii)
mg/L/h (volumetric PHB productivity close to zero in phase iii)
mg/L/h (volumetric biomass productivity, drastically reduced, phase iii)
Y_CO2/CO = 0.745 g/g (off-gas)
Y_H2/CO = 0.036 g/g (off-gas)

growth_records.cite: Karmann et al. Fed-Batch Cultivations of Rhodospirillum rubrum Under Multiple Nutrient-Limited Growth Conditions on Syngas as a Novel Option to Produce Poly(3-Hydroxybutyrate) (PHB). Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00059. [PMC6454858]
growth_records.snippet: during C,N-limited growth (starting 100 h after inoculation), the culture exhibited a drastically reduced volumetric biomass productivity of only approximately 10 mg L-1 h-1, reducing the volumetric PHB productivity close to zero even thoug
growth_records.notes: C,N-limited fed-batch, phase (iii). NH4Cl reduced 1 g/L -> 383 mg/L (100 mg/L NH4-N). Reported: volumetric biomass productivity ~10 mg/L/h; q_PHB=1.95 mg/gDW/h; volumetric PHB ~0; final CDW 2.95+/-0.06 g/L; PHB 0.32 g/L (11.1% w/w); TCC 3.4e12/L. This condition consumed the MOST acetate per gDW/h (Figure 7C, numeric only in figure) because little biomass formed per acetate. Yields (Table 2): Y_X/acetate=0.353, Y_X/CO=0.020, Y_PHB/acetate=0.038, Y_PHB/CO=0.002, Y_H2/CO=0.036, Y_CO2/CO=0.745, Y_X/N=17.65 (g/g). DERIVED volumetric acetate uptake = 10 mg/L/h / 0.353 = 28.3 mg acetate/L/h (is_experimental:false). q_PHB molar = 1.95/86 = 0.0227 mmol/gDW/h. mu=0.1/h, td=6.93 h from shared batch phase.

up.units: mg acetate/L/h (volumetric, DERIVED)
up.method: calculated from volumetric biomass productivity / Y_X/acetate

up.units: Y_X/CO = 0.053 g/g (CO consumed; highest CO->biomass yield of the three)

sec.units: mg/gDW/h (specific PHB productivity q_PHB, phase iii; highest of the three runs)
mg/L/h (volumetric PHB productivity, phase iii; highest of the three)
mg/L/h (volumetric biomass productivity, phase iii; highest of the three)
Y_CO2/CO = 0.941 g/g (off-gas)
Y_H2/CO = 0.040 g/g (off-gas)

growth_records.cite: Karmann et al. Fed-Batch Cultivations of Rhodospirillum rubrum Under Multiple Nutrient-Limited Growth Conditions on Syngas as a Novel Option to Produce Poly(3-Hydroxybutyrate) (PHB). Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00059. [PMC6454858]
growth_records.snippet: during C,P limitation (starting 125 h after inoculation) we measured the highest biomass productivity of 38 mg L-1 h-1 and, with 13.5 mg L-1 h-1, and 5.6 mg g-1 h-1 the highest volumetric and specific PHB production rates... final CDW 5.32
growth_records.notes: C,P-limited fed-batch, phase (iii); best performer (PHB 30% w/w). Phosphate buffer omitted, yeast extract sole P source (some P bound in yeast extract may be inaccessible, so P-limitation may onset earlier than measured). Reported: volumetric biomass productivity 38 mg/L/h; volumetric PHB 13.5 mg/L/h; q_PHB=5.6 mg/gDW/h; final CDW 5.32+/-0.09 g/L; PHB 1.6 g/L (30.0% w/w); TCC 2.7e12/L. Yields (Table 2): Y_X/acetate=0.681, Y_X/CO=0.053, Y_PHB/acetate=0.204, Y_PHB/CO=0.016, Y_H2/CO=0.040, Y_CO2/CO=0.941, Y_X/P=112 (g/g). DERIVED volumetric acetate uptake = 38 mg/L/h / 0.681 = 55.8 mg acetate/L/h (is_experimental:false). q_PHB molar = 5.6/86 = 0.0651 mmol/gDW/h. PHB productivity ~5x higher than C-limited over same window. mu=0.1/h, td=6.93 h from shared batch phase.

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
